cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out why Ms. Leibfried asked the author to recite a passage in front of the class. Let me go through the text again to pick up the clues. The author mentions that many of their classmates disliked novels because they were "not real," but they didn't care what others thought. Ms. Leibfried noticed the author's interest in reading and writing and made them feel less shy. She even suggested a book. The big project was a book report to be read aloud, but Ms. Leibfried suggested the author do something "a little different" and recite a passage from "The Bell Jar." The author was nervous but after the recitation, the class applauded, and the teacher praised them for choosing a sensitive piece and delivering it beautifully. The author felt confident for the first time. Looking at the options: A) Test memory. The passage doesn't mention anything about
